column4: The Eucharist is the body and blood of Christ
column6: The eucharistic elements are identified as the flesh and blood of Jesus

column4: Christians gather on Sunday for the Eucharist
column6: The Lord's Day is the regular day for eucharistic assembly

column6: Proper eucharistic celebration is administered by or authorized by the bishop

column5: Baptism instruction: baptize in the name of Father, Son, Holy Spirit in running water; if not available use other water; if not cold then warm; if neither pour water three times on the head. Candidate and baptizer fast beforehand.
column9: Direct baptismal instruction with flexible water accommodation

column5: Eucharist thanksgiving over cup (holy vine of David) and broken bread (life and knowledge through Jesus). Let none eat or drink except those baptized in the Lord's name.
column9: Early eucharistic prayer with baptism prerequisite

column5: On the Lord's Day come together, break bread, hold Eucharist after confessing transgressions so offering may be pure. Let none with quarrel join until reconciled, that sacrifice not be defiled. "In every place and time offer me a pure sacrifice."
column9: Sunday eucharist with sacrifice language and reconciliation requirement

column5: They abstain from Eucharist and prayer because they confess not the Eucharist to be the flesh of our Saviour Jesus Christ which suffered for our sins and which the Father raised.
column9: Anti-docetist eucharistic realism, explicit flesh language

column5: See that you all follow the bishop. Let that be deemed a proper Eucharist which is administered either by the bishop or by one to whom he has entrusted it. Wherever the bishop shall appear, there let the multitude also be. It is not lawful without the bishop either to baptize or to celebrate.
column9: Episcopal control over eucharist and baptism

column5: Take heed, then, often to come together to give thanks to God and show forth His praise. For when you assemble frequently in the same place, the powers of Satan are destroyed.

Obey the bishop and presbytery with undivided mind, breaking one and the same bread, which is the medicine of immortality and the antidote to prevent us from dying but which causes that we should live forever in Jesus Christ.

Those who believe and undertake to live accordingly are brought where there is water and regenerated in the name of God the Father, our Saviour Jesus Christ, and the Holy Spirit. This washing is called illumination.
column9: Detailed baptism description with trinitarian formula

column5: After baptism we bring the person to the assembly for prayers. Bread and cup of wine mixed with water are brought to the president who gives thanks. This food is called Eucharist, of which only baptized may partake. Not as common bread and drink do we receive these, but as the flesh and blood of Jesus who was made flesh.
column9: Full description of post-baptismal eucharist with real-presence language

column5: On the day called Sunday all gather to one place. Memoirs of apostles or writings of prophets are read, the president instructs, then all pray together. Bread and wine with water are brought, the president offers prayers and thanks, the people say Amen, and there is distribution to each.
column9: Sunday assembly pattern with eucharist description
